TechCrunch.title: After pivoting, Y Combinator grad Glimpse raises $35M led by a16z
TechCrunch.summary: Glimpse announced on Wednesday a $35 million Series A led by Andreessen Horowitz, with participation from 8VC and YC.

TechCrunch.title: Harbinger’s next product will be hybrid emergency vehicles
TechCrunch.summary: The young California startup is already building RVs, FedEx vans, and box trucks. Now its hybrid powertrain will underpin ambulances and mobile healthcare units.

TechCrunch.title: Arinna raises $4M seed round to solve the space power problem
TechCrunch.summary: Arinna builds solar cells specifically for spacecraft and has developed an ultrathin new material that it expects to be far more flexible and more efficient than current technology.

TechCrunch.title: With Sift Stack, two ex-SpaceX engineers are bringing the software that helped launch rockets to the factory floor
TechCrunch.summary: Sift is building the data infrastructure for advanced manufacturing.

TechCrunch.title: Lucid Bots raises $20M to keep up with demand for its window-washing drones
TechCrunch.summary: Lucid Bots has seen demand accelerate over the last year for its window cleaning drones and power washing robots.

TechCrunch.title: A former Thiel fellow’s startup just launched a drone it says can replace police helicopters
TechCrunch.summary: Brinc launched its newest 911 response drone this week, as the company seeks to expand its prominence in the public safety industry.

TechCrunch.title: Amazon just bought a startup making kid-size humanoid robots
TechCrunch.summary: The acquisition of Fauna Robotics is the second robotics startup Amazon has purchased this month.

TechCrunch.title: With $3.5B in fresh capital, Kleiner Perkins is going all in on AI
TechCrunch.summary: The fundraise includes $1 billion for investing in early-stage startups, and $2.5 billion for late-stage growth businesses.

TechCrunch.title: New Mexico just handed Meta its first courtroom defeat over child safety, and the rest of the country is watching
TechCrunch.summary: The dollar amount isn’t as important as the fact that this is the first jury verdict of its kind against Meta over harm to young people.

TechCrunch.title: Lululemon bets Epoch Biodesign can eat its shorts, literally
TechCrunch.summary: Startup Epoch Biodesign uses enzymes to break plastic waste down into monomers, the chemical building blocks that can be used to make new plastic.

TechCrunch.title: OpenAI’s Sora was the creepiest app on your phone — now it’s shutting down
TechCrunch.summary: Though the underlying Sora 2 video- and audio-generation model is scarily impressive, there was not sustained interest in an AI-only social feed.

TechCrunch.title: Accel, Prosus pick six ‘off-the-map’ startups for inaugural India cohort
TechCrunch.summary: The six startups were selected from over 2,000 applications and will receive $500,000 to $2 million each.

TechCrunch.title: Kentucky woman rejects $26M offer to turn her farm into a data center
TechCrunch.summary: A "major artificial intelligence company" reportedly offered a Kentucky family $26 million to build a data center on their farm.

TechCrunch.title: Anthropic hands Claude Code more control, but keeps it on a leash
